chumsky0.10.1

Cached

Required Associated Types

Required Methods

Dyn Compatibility

Implementors

In chumsky::cache

chumsky::cache

Trait Cached 

Source 
pub trait Cached {
    type Parser<'src>;

    // Required method
    fn make_parser<'src>(self) -> Self::Parser<'src>;
}
Expand description

Implementing this trait allows you to cache parsers for use with inputs of different lifetimes, avoiding the need to recreate the parser for each input lifetime.

Required Associated Types§

Source

type Parser<'src>

The type of the parser to be cached.

Because parsers tend to have unwieldy types, it is recommended to perform type erasure here. For example, a parser with input type &'src str and output type Token<'src> might have one of the following types.

ⓘ
Boxed<'src, 'src, &'src str, Token<'src>, extra::Default>
Arc<dyn Parser<'src, &'src str, Token<'src>, extra::Default> + Send + Sync + 'src>

Required Methods§

Source

fn make_parser<'src>(self) -> Self::Parser<'src>

Create an instance of the parser

Dyn Compatibility§

This trait is not dyn compatible.

In older versions of Rust, dyn compatibility was called "object safety", so this trait is not object safe.

Implementors§
